Non GMO Project Verified; NAOOA Certified Quality seal

Cold processed

Origin: Italy, Greece, and Spain

Suitable for vegans, non-GMO

$6.99/33.8 fl oz [1,000 ml] dark glass bottle (approx. 67 servings)
Description1: Aroma/flavor reflected mostly ripe olive fruit with the primary descriptor of nutty. Other descriptors included buttery, green banana and green olive with hints of ripe banana and woody. Also noted was a "toasted" aroma. Medium astringency was noted.

Low cost, CL Approved medium extra virgin olive oil

All natural cold pressed

Origin: Italy, Spain, Argentina, and Greece

$7.99/32 fl oz [946 ml] dark glass bottle (approx. 63 servings)
Description1: Aroma/flavor reflected mostly green olive fruit with the primary descriptor of herbaceous. Other descriptors included green olive, butter and floral with hints of green tea, nutty and stone fruit. Medium astringency was noted.
1 Sensory analyses were performed by Nancy Ash, a professional olive oil taste tester who is Deputy Taste Panel Leader for the California Olive Oil Council and certified as a Taste Panel Supervisor by the Organizzazione Nazoinale Assaggiatori Olio di Oliva in Imperia, Italy. Although an expert opinion, it is only from one individual and does not constitute a full sensory panel (at least five trained testers) as would be required for an assessment to qualify under USDA standards. Samples were submitted blind (with no producer information), labeled only with a sample number. The following are desirable traits: "Fruity" (aroma of fresh olive fruit); "Bitter" (indicative of fresh olive fruit); "Pungent" (stinging sensation in the throat, similar to "peppery"). Defects noted on sensory analysis include: "Fusty" (suggests fermentation during storage before milling); "Rancid" (stale); "Heated/Burnt" (from processing at too high a temperature); "Rough" (pasty, thick, greasy mouth feel); winey (sour/vinegary flavor).
2 Purity was determined based on fatty acid content to distinguish olive oil from other seed oils.
3 Rancidity (oxidation) was determined based on two tests: The peroxide value (shown above), which reflects primary oxidation of the oil and a peroxide value above 20 indicates a rancid product and 2) the difference, or delta k, in UV absorbance by the oil of two specific wavelengths of light, which indicates the quantity of oxidized compounds present in the oil — values above 0.01 indicate a problem. All products passed both tests.  
4 Not tested but claimed on label. Origins of oils may vary for a given brand over time, particularly blended from multiple countries.
Unless otherwise noted, information about the products listed above is based on the samples purchased by ConsumerLab.com (CL) for this Product Review. Manufacturers may change ingredients and label information at any time, so be sure to check labels carefully when evaluating the products you use or buy. If a product's ingredients differ from what is listed above, it may not necessarily be of the same quality as what was tested.

The information contained in this report is based on the compilation and review of information from product labeling and analytic testing. CL applies what it believes to be the most appropriate testing methods and standards. The information in this report does not reflect the opinion or recommendation of CL, its officers or employees. CL cannot assure the accuracy of information.
